points: Equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the centre. Conversely, equal central angles correspond to equal chords.
The perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord. Conversely, the line from the centre bisecting a chord is perpendicular to it.
Equal chords are equidistant from the centre. Conversely, chords equidistant from the centre are equal in length.

summary.category: Arcs and Angles at the Circumference
points: Equal chords subtend congruent arcs; congruent arcs correspond to equal chords.
The angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining arc (∠POQ = 2∠PAQ).
Angles in the same segment of a circle are equal — any two points on the same arc subtending the same chord form equal angles.
The angle subtended by a diameter at any point on the circle is always 90° (angle in a semicircle).
A cyclic quadrilateral is a quadrilateral whose all four vertices lie on the same circle. The sum of opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral is 180°.
If the sum of opposite angles in a quadrilateral is 180°, then the quadrilateral is cyclic.
If a line segment joining two points subtends equal angles at two other points on the same side of the line, then all four points lie on a circle (are concyclic).
